title: pleopeltis elmeri
intro: Pleopeltis elmeri, also known as Elmer's polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the Philippines. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Pleopeltis elmeri include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to improve respiratory and digestive health. Herbal preparations of Pleopeltis elmeri can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant in traditional remedies such as ointments and salves.
